for f in /workspace/kubernetes--v1.32.0/pkg/registry/admissionregistration/rest/storage_apiserver.go \
  /workspace/kubernetes--v1.32.0/pkg/registry/authentication/rest/storage_authentication.go \
  /workspace/kubernetes--v1.32.0/pkg/registry/resource/rest/storage_resource.go \
  /workspace/kubernetes--v1.32.0/pkg/registry/storage/rest/storage_storage.go; do
  echo "=== $f ==="; grep -n "func.*[Vv]1\|func.*[Vv]1beta1\|StorageVersion\|v1beta1.*v1\|v1.*v1beta1" "$f" | head -30; echo; done

  "text": "This analysis covers the Kubernetes v1.32.0 codebase for files and symbols involved in registering API group storage versions mapping v1beta1 to v1 equivalents.\n\n## REST Storage Providers with both v1beta1Storage() and v1Storage()\n\nThree REST storage providers in `pkg/registry/` define both `v1beta1Storage()` and `v1Storage()` methods, enabling serving both API versions while using the same underlying etcd storage:\n\n1. **`pkg/registry/admissionregistration/rest/storage_apiserver.go`** (`RESTStorageProvider`): Registers `admissionregistration.k8s.io` group resources. `v1Storage()` handles ValidatingWebhookConfiguration, MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, and ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding for v1. `v1beta1Storage()` handles ValidatingAdmissionPolicy and ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding for v1beta1. Both v1 and v1beta1 resources share the same underlying storage objects.\n\n2. **`pkg/registry/authentication/rest/storage_authentication.go`** (`RESTStorageProvider`): Registers `authentication.k8s.io` group resources. `v1Storage()` handles TokenReview and SelfSubjectReview for v1. `v1beta1Storage()` handles SelfSubjectReview for v1beta1 — both backed by the same `selfsubjectreview.NewREST()` storage.\n\n3. **`pkg/registry/storage/rest/storage_storage.go`** (`RESTStorageProvider`): Registers `storage.k8s.io` group resources. `v1beta1Storage()` handles VolumeAttributesClass for v1beta1 (using `volumeattributesclassstore.NewREST()`). `v1Storage()` handles StorageClass, VolumeAttachment, CSINode, CSIDriver, and CSIStorageCapacity for v1.\n\n## REST Storage Provider with only v1beta1Storage()\n\n4. **`pkg/registry/resource/rest/storage_resource.go`** (`RESTStorageProvider`): Registers `resource.k8s.io` group resources for v1beta1 (DeviceClass, ResourceClaim, ResourceClaimTemplate, ResourceSlice). Also has `v1alpha3Storage()` but no v1Storage() yet — the resources are in beta and haven't graduated to v1.\n\n## StorageVersion Manager Interface\n\nThe `Manager` interface in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion/manager.go`** defines how API servers track and update storage versions:\n- `Manager` interface with methods: `AddResourceInfo`, `UpdateStorageVersions`, `PendingUpdate`, `LastUpdateError`, `Completed`\n- `defaultManager` struct implements `Manager`\n- `NewDefaultManager()` creates the default implementation\n- `ResourceInfo` struct holds per-resource info including `EncodingVersion`, `EquivalentResourceMapper`, `DirectlyDecodableVersions`, and `ServedVersions`\n\nThe companion file **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion/updater.go`** provides:\n- `updateStorageVersionFor()` — updates the StorageVersion CRD object for a resource\n- `localUpdateStorageVersion()` — local in-memory update to the StorageVersion object\n- `SetCommonEncodingVersion()` — sets the common encoding version when all servers agree\n\nThe Manager is wired into `GenericAPIServer` in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go`** (field `StorageVersionManager`) and initialized in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go`** via `storageversion.NewDefaultManager()`.\n\nThe `WithStorageVersionPrecondition` handler in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/storageversion.go`** blocks write requests until storage versions are updated, consuming the Manager.\n\n## ResourceEncodingConfig\n\nThe `ResourceEncodingConfig` interface in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage/resource_encoding_config.go`** controls which GroupVersion is used for etcd storage:\n- `ResourceEncodingConfig` interface: `StorageEncodingFor(GroupResource)` and `InMemoryEncodingFor(GroupResource)`\n- `CompatibilityResourceEncodingConfig` interface: `BackwardCompatibileStorageEncodingFor(GroupResource, Object)` — for emulation versioning\n- `DefaultResourceEncodingConfig` struct: stores per-resource overrides; `SetResourceEncoding()` sets explicit mappings\n- `OverridingResourceEncoding` struct: holds `ExternalResourceEncoding` (etcd storage GV) and `InternalResourceEncoding`\n\nThe `DefaultStorageFactory` in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage/storage_factory.go`** uses `ResourceEncodingConfig` in its `NewConfig()` method to determine the actual storage version (codec) for each resource when talking to etcd.\n\n`MergeResourceEncodingConfigs()` in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/resourceconfig/helpers.go`** merges override storage encodings (e.g., from `--storage-versions` flag) into the `DefaultResourceEncodingConfig`.\n\n## Control Plane Configuration\n\nIn **`pkg/controlplane/instance.go`**, `betaAPIGroupVersionsDisabledByDefault` lists all v1beta1 group versions that are served but disabled by default (including `admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1`, `authentication.k8s.io/v1beta1`, `storage.k8s.io/v1beta1`, `resource.k8s.io/v1beta1`, `networking.k8s.io/v1beta1`, and flowcontrol v1beta1-v1beta3). The `DefaultAPIResourceConfigSource()` function enables v1 (GA) versions by default while disabling these beta versions.\n\nIn **`pkg/controlplane/apiserver/aggregator.go`**, the kube-aggregator uses `v1.SchemeGroupVersion` as the primary storage codec (with v1beta1 as fallback) for `apiregistration.k8s.io` resources — this is the v1beta1→v1 migration in action for APIService objects.\n\nIn **`pkg/controlplane/apiserver/apiextensions.go`**, the apiextensions server reverses this pattern, using `v1beta1.SchemeGroupVersion` as the primary storage codec for CRDs (with v1 as fallback) for backward compatibility with large CRD objects.\n\n## REST StorageVersionProvider Interface\n\nThe `StorageVersionProvider` interface in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go`** is an optional interface that storage objects can implement to return a `runtime.GroupVersioner` indicating which group version they store objects as — this is used to populate the `ResourceInfo.EncodingVersion` field registered with the StorageVersion manager."

## REST Storage Providers with v1beta1Storage() and v1Storage() Methods

Three files in `pkg/registry/` define both methods, enabling dual-version serving with shared storage:

1. **`pkg/registry/admissionregistration/rest/storage_apiserver.go`** — `admissionregistration.k8s.io` group (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy etc. served in both v1 and v1beta1)
2. **`pkg/registry/authentication/rest/storage_authentication.go`** — `authentication.k8s.io` group (SelfSubjectReview served in v1 and v1beta1)

- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion/updater.go`** — `updateStorageVersionFor()`, `localUpdateStorageVersion()`

## ResourceEncodingConfig
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage/resource_encoding_config.go`** — `ResourceEncodingConfig` interface, `DefaultResourceEncodingConfig`, `SetResourceEncoding()`
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage/storage_factory.go`** — `DefaultStorageFactory` uses `ResourceEncodingConfig` to pick the etcd storage version
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/resourceconfig/helpers.go`** — `MergeResourceEncodingConfigs()`

    19→import (

    25→	apiextensionsinformers "k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/client/informers/externalversions/apiextensions/v1"

    32→	genericapiserver "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server"
    33→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz"
    34→	utilfeature "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature"

    45→	informers "k8s.io/kube-aggregator/pkg/client/informers/externalversions/apiregistration/v1"

    52→func CreateAggregatorConfig(
    53→	kubeAPIServerConfig genericapiserver.Config,
    54→	commandOptions options.CompletedOptions,
    55→	externalInformers kubeexternalinformers.SharedInformerFactory,
    56→	serviceResolver aggregatorapiserver.ServiceResolver,
    57→	proxyTransport *http.Transport,
    58→	peerProxy utilpeerproxy.Interface,
    59→	pluginInitializers []admission.PluginInitializer,
    60→) (*aggregatorapiserver.Config, error) {
    61→	// make a shallow copy to let us twiddle a few things
    62→	// most of the config actually remains the same.  We only need to mess with a couple items related to the particulars of the aggregator
    63→	genericConfig := kubeAPIServerConfig
    64→	genericConfig.PostStartHooks = map[string]genericapiserver.PostStartHookConfigEntry{}
    65→	genericConfig.RESTOptionsGetter = nil
    66→	// prevent generic API server from installing the OpenAPI handler. Aggregator server
    67→	// has its own customized OpenAPI handler.
    68→	genericConfig.SkipOpenAPIInstallation = true
    69→
    70→	if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.StorageVersionAPI) &&
    71→		utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerIdentity) {
    72→		// Add StorageVersionPrecondition handler to aggregator-apiserver.
    73→		// The handler will block write requests to built-in resources until the
    74→		// target resources' storage versions are up-to-date.
    75→		genericConfig.BuildHandlerChainFunc = genericapiserver.BuildHandlerChainWithStorageVersionPrecondition
    76→	}
    77→
    78→	if peerProxy != nil {
    79→		originalHandlerChainBuilder := genericConfig.BuildHandlerChainFunc
    80→		genericConfig.BuildHandlerChainFunc = func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *genericapiserver.Config) http.Handler {
    81→			// Add peer proxy handler to aggregator-apiserver.
    82→			// wrap the peer proxy handler first.
    83→			apiHandler = peerProxy.WrapHandler(apiHandler)
    84→			return originalHandlerChainBuilder(apiHandler, c)
    85→		}
    86→	}
    87→
    88→	// copy the etcd options so we don't mutate originals.
    89→	// we assume that the etcd options have been completed already.  avoid messing with anything outside
    90→	// of changes to StorageConfig as that may lead to unexpected behavior when the options are applied.
    91→	etcdOptions := *commandOptions.Etcd
    92→	etcdOptions.StorageConfig.Codec = aggregatorscheme.Codecs.LegacyCodec(v1.SchemeGroupVersion, v1beta1.SchemeGroupVersion)
    93→	etcdOptions.StorageConfig.EncodeVersioner = runtime.NewMultiGroupVersioner(v1.SchemeGroupVersion, schema.GroupKind{Group: v1beta1.GroupName})
    94→	etcdOptions.SkipHealthEndpoints = true // avoid double wiring of health checks
    95→	if err := etcdOptions.ApplyTo(&genericConfig); err != nil {
    96→		return nil, err
    97→	}
    98→
    99→	// override MergedResourceConfig with aggregator defaults and registry
   100→	if err := commandOptions.APIEnablement.ApplyTo(
   101→		&genericConfig,
   102→		aggregatorapiserver.DefaultAPIResourceConfigSource(),
   103→		aggregatorscheme.Scheme); err != nil {
   104→		return nil, err
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	aggregatorConfig := &aggregatorapiserver.Config{
   108→		GenericConfig: &genericapiserver.RecommendedConfig{
   109→			Config:                genericConfig,
   110→			SharedInformerFactory: externalInformers,
   111→		},
   112→		ExtraConfig: aggregatorapiserver.ExtraConfig{
   113→			ProxyClientCertFile:       commandOptions.ProxyClientCertFile,
   114→			ProxyClientKeyFile:        commandOptions.ProxyClientKeyFile,
   115→			PeerAdvertiseAddress:      commandOptions.PeerAdvertiseAddress,
   116→			ServiceResolver:           serviceResolver,
   117→			ProxyTransport:            proxyTransport,
   118→			RejectForwardingRedirects: commandOptions.AggregatorRejectForwardingRedirects,
   119→		},
   120→	}

  "text": "This analysis covers the Kubernetes v1.32.0 codebase for files and symbols involved in registering API group storage versions mapping v1beta1 to v1 equivalents.\n\n## REST Storage Providers with both v1beta1Storage() and v1Storage()\n\nThree REST storage providers in `pkg/registry/` define both `v1beta1Storage()` and `v1Storage()` methods, enabling serving both API versions while using the same underlying etcd storage:\n\n1. **`pkg/registry/admissionregistration/rest/storage_apiserver.go`** (`RESTStorageProvider`): Registers `admissionregistration.k8s.io` group resources. `v1Storage()` handles ValidatingWebhookConfiguration, MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, and ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding for v1. `v1beta1Storage()` handles ValidatingAdmissionPolicy and ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding for v1beta1. Both v1 and v1beta1 resources share the same underlying storage objects.\n\n2. **`pkg/registry/authentication/rest/storage_authentication.go`** (`RESTStorageProvider`): Registers `authentication.k8s.io` group resources. `v1Storage()` handles TokenReview and SelfSubjectReview for v1. `v1beta1Storage()` handles SelfSubjectReview for v1beta1 — both backed by the same `selfsubjectreview.NewREST()` storage.\n\n3. **`pkg/registry/storage/rest/storage_storage.go`** (`RESTStorageProvider`): Registers `storage.k8s.io` group resources. `v1beta1Storage()` handles VolumeAttributesClass for v1beta1 (using `volumeattributesclassstore.NewREST()`). `v1Storage()` handles StorageClass, VolumeAttachment, CSINode, CSIDriver, and CSIStorageCapacity for v1.\n\n## REST Storage Provider with only v1beta1Storage()\n\n4. **`pkg/registry/resource/rest/storage_resource.go`** (`RESTStorageProvider`): Registers `resource.k8s.io` group resources for v1beta1 (DeviceClass, ResourceClaim, ResourceClaimTemplate, ResourceSlice). Also has `v1alpha3Storage()` but no v1Storage() yet — the resources are in beta and haven't graduated to v1.\n\n## StorageVersion Manager Interface\n\nThe `Manager` interface in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion/manager.go`** defines how API servers track and update storage versions:\n- `Manager` interface with methods: `AddResourceInfo`, `UpdateStorageVersions`, `PendingUpdate`, `LastUpdateError`, `Completed`\n- `defaultManager` struct implements `Manager`\n- `NewDefaultManager()` creates the default implementation\n- `ResourceInfo` struct holds per-resource info including `EncodingVersion`, `EquivalentResourceMapper`, `DirectlyDecodableVersions`, and `ServedVersions`\n\nThe companion file **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion/updater.go`** provides:\n- `updateStorageVersionFor()` — updates the StorageVersion CRD object for a resource\n- `localUpdateStorageVersion()` — local in-memory update to the StorageVersion object\n- `SetCommonEncodingVersion()` — sets the common encoding version when all servers agree\n\nThe Manager is wired into `GenericAPIServer` in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go`** (field `StorageVersionManager`) and initialized in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go`** via `storageversion.NewDefaultManager()`.\n\nThe `WithStorageVersionPrecondition` handler in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/storageversion.go`** blocks write requests until storage versions are updated, consuming the Manager.\n\n## ResourceEncodingConfig\n\nThe `ResourceEncodingConfig` interface in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage/resource_encoding_config.go`** controls which GroupVersion is used for etcd storage:\n- `ResourceEncodingConfig` interface: `StorageEncodingFor(GroupResource)` and `InMemoryEncodingFor(GroupResource)`\n- `CompatibilityResourceEncodingConfig` interface: `BackwardCompatibileStorageEncodingFor(GroupResource, Object)` — for emulation versioning\n- `DefaultResourceEncodingConfig` struct: stores per-resource overrides; `SetResourceEncoding()` sets explicit mappings\n- `OverridingResourceEncoding` struct: holds `ExternalResourceEncoding` (etcd storage GV) and `InternalResourceEncoding`\n\nThe `DefaultStorageFactory` in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage/storage_factory.go`** uses `ResourceEncodingConfig` in its `NewConfig()` method to determine the actual storage version (codec) for each resource when talking to etcd.\n\n`MergeResourceEncodingConfigs()` in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/resourceconfig/helpers.go`** merges override storage encodings (e.g., from `--storage-versions` flag) into the `DefaultResourceEncodingConfig`.\n\n## Control Plane Configuration\n\nIn **`pkg/controlplane/instance.go`**, `betaAPIGroupVersionsDisabledByDefault` lists all v1beta1 group versions that are served but disabled by default (including `admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1`, `authentication.k8s.io/v1beta1`, `storage.k8s.io/v1beta1`, `resource.k8s.io/v1beta1`, `networking.k8s.io/v1beta1`, and flowcontrol v1beta1-v1beta3). The `DefaultAPIResourceConfigSource()` function enables v1 (GA) versions by default while disabling these beta versions.\n\nIn **`pkg/controlplane/apiserver/aggregator.go`**, the kube-aggregator uses `v1.SchemeGroupVersion` as the primary storage codec (with v1beta1 as fallback) for `apiregistration.k8s.io` resources — this is the v1beta1→v1 migration in action for APIService objects.\n\nIn **`pkg/controlplane/apiserver/apiextensions.go`**, the apiextensions server reverses this pattern, using `v1beta1.SchemeGroupVersion` as the primary storage codec for CRDs (with v1 as fallback) for backward compatibility with large CRD objects.\n\n## REST StorageVersionProvider Interface\n\nThe `StorageVersionProvider` interface in **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go`** is an optional interface that storage objects can implement to return a `runtime.GroupVersioner` indicating which group version they store objects as — this is used to populate the `ResourceInfo.EncodingVersion` field registered with the StorageVersion manager."
